title: What an American thinks about Montenegro - Bike touring 2010

transcript.text: What an american thinks about montenegro um I'd never really like heard of montenegro
But um yeah I crossed it into it from bosnia and then I went straight up and uh in this really
Intense valley and um looked way down the river went across this bridge it was like way above the
River like a couple hundred feet above the river and then I went through all these all these like
Tunnels that day I rode through like a hundred tunnels and I went down this like uh this this
Reservoir it was just lake in this you know like really intense valley um for like forever it was
Like 30 it was like 30 miles long or something or 20 miles long it was really long and then it split
And went off the other way and then I followed one way and then went up and I could see these you
Know stuck half mountains on our side so it was like from the beginning it was really intense and I went
Up this path and I went down into the city and I stayed at a hotel and I could I noticed the hotel
Was like really run down like um the hotel that's the room I stayed in it was 37 euro I shouldn't I
Shouldn't have done it I was a sissy because it was running really hard when I went in I was really
Tired but um but yeah they had like you know the electric cable for the tv like wasn't even plugged
In so I had to like plug it in you know with the the multiplier that wasn't plugged in and then
Playing with the dog the dog ears and there were only a couple channels and they didn't they said
They said they had wi-fi but they didn't it didn't work so then he gave me this cable to plug it in
The wall but there was no I had to sit on the floor because there was no chair there and that didn't
Work so and like they put me on a bike in the storage room and it was really muggy and people
Looked really like depressed and like quiet and like subdued or whatever and I was like oh this place is
Weird but then I went into pogorice the next day and I kind of noticed that it was like montenegro
Is like the country in the balkans and former yugoslavia that is the most looks like the most like
First world of any of the countries like more than um you know any of those other countries even more
Than like um you know czech or anywhere since you know or even probably seemed like even more than like

But um you know they had the wi-fi and the rest of them and stuff but I don't know just people looked
Like whiter and they were dressing in really nice clothes and stylish like I mean like like it might
As well have been in america I mean yeah their skin was a little bit more dark though they had like
You know like bone structure of the white person but like skin it was dark so they're really good
